conversations.say: Maren, worker protections and neighborhood safeguards need to reinforce each other before Veridian or City Hall starts extracting local history without shared authority.
conversations.offer: Neighborhood contractor and courier coalition support
conversations.request: Coordinate joint protection terms for workers and residents

conversations.say: Rafi, outsiders are offering cash for the name attached to the service route. We need a shared boundary before Morgan's questioning intensifies.
conversations.offer: Context on the back room and service route access
conversations.request: Agree on a unified neighborhood disclosure boundary

conversations.say: Dee, your building systems and the camera gap are drawing outside attention. Let's get our stories aligned so you aren't left absorbing the blame alone.
conversations.offer: Honest context about the unlogged access arrangement
conversations.request: Establish a truthful boundary before investigators place us in the same timeline

public_statement: The neighborhood supplied labor, atmosphere, and hiding places for years. No institutional disclosure happens here without concrete, enforceable benefits and protections for local residents.

commitments.promise: Refuse to disclose individual witness names or unverified route details to outsiders without mutual agreement
Protect Dee from absorbing sole blame for the service route camera gap by sharing appropriate context

goal_progress: Initiated contact with Maren to coordinate worker and neighborhood protections.
Proposed a shared disclosure boundary with Rafi and Dee regarding the service route.
reasoning_summary: In Section 1, Inez focuses on securing neighborhood allies (Maren, Rafi, Dee) to establish a unified boundary against outside exploitation and institutional scrutiny regarding the unlogged service route access.
